claims.text: On 25 June 2026 a magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck 16 km SW of Moron in northern Venezuela, west of Caracas.
The USGS is assessing earthquake-triggered landslide hazards generated by the 2026 Venezuela earthquake sequence.

ReliefWeb is publishing a running series of Venezuela earthquake response situation reports (through at least Situation Report #7, dated 30 June 2026), coordinated via its dedicated disaster tracking page for this event.

description: A magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck northern Venezuela west of Caracas on 25 June 2026, 39 seconds after a magnitude 7.2 foreshock in the same sequence. The USGS assessed that high casualties and widespread damage are probable and is tracking aftershocks and earthquake-triggered landslide hazards. ReliefWeb is publishing an ongoing series of humanitarian situation reports on the response.

impact.summary: USGS assessed high casualties and widespread damage as probable. Confirmed casualty figures are still being established; ReliefWeb's running situation-report series and the USGS event page carry the current assessment.

location.region: Near Morón / San Felipe, northern Venezuela (west of Caracas)

name: Venezuela M7.5 Earthquake Doublet
